title: Gum on Denim
timeEstimate: 20–40 minutes; freeze + crack + Goo Gone, indigo-safe with hidden test
stainChemistry: Chewing gum is a sticky polymer plus softeners and sweeteners. The polymer doesn't dissolve in water — it has to be frozen brittle so it cracks off mechanically, or dissolved with a citrus / petroleum solvent. Warm gum is soft and spreads when touched, making it worse. Cold gum is brittle and lifts cleanly. On denim, three things matter: indigo dye sits on the surface and any solvent can lift color along with the residue, the weave is heavy enough that gum sits on top rather than penetrating fiber (good news), and aggressive scrubbing leaves a halo on dark denim. Don't pick at warm gum with fingernails — it spreads and pushes the polymer deeper into the weave.
whyThisWorks: Freezing makes the gum polymer brittle and breaks its grip on the denim. Cracking with a plastic edge lifts most of it off in pieces. Goo Gone (citrus solvent) on a cloth dissolves residual stickiness without the dye-loss risk of acetone. Cool wash plus air-dry finishes. The honest cap on denim: tightly-set gum on raw / selvage denim or aggressive removal attempts often leave a permanent indent or color loss; pro is the right call there.
defaultAssumption: Chewing gum on light-wash or medium-wash denim — sat on it at the park, kid stuck it under the table. Fresh = freeze + lift = high success. Raw / selvage / black overdyed denim = pro from minute one (any solvent + scrubbing risks color loss).

spottingProtocol.instruction: Don't pick at warm gum with fingernails — it spreads and pushes the polymer deeper into the weave. Stop, freeze, then lift. Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim = pro from minute one.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Ice in a sealed plastic bag
spottingProtocol.instruction: Place a sealed bag of ice on the gum 15–20 minutes. NEVER direct ice on denim — water spotting causes its own ring. The gum should be hard and brittle when ready.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Plastic card, plastic spoon, or dull butter knife
spottingProtocol.instruction: Crack the frozen gum with the edge of a plastic card. Lift off pieces. Never gouge denim — flat-edge work only. Most surface gum comes off this way.
Inside seam: dab Goo Gone on a hidden seam, blot with white cloth. If indigo transfers, skip Goo Gone — Dawn-only finish. If clean, solvent is safe to use on the visible spot.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Goo Gone on white cloth, ventilation
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply Goo Gone to a clean white cloth — never pour on denim. Tamp the residue, flip cloth as it picks up sticky transfer. Outside-in. 2 passes. Open windows; flammable, no flame.
Dawn + cool water on damp cloth, work the spot 5 min to clear Goo Gone residue. Cool machine wash, normal detergent. AIR-DRY. Inspect dry.

steps: 1. Don't pick at warm gum. Freeze first.
2. Ice-bag freeze 15–20 min — never direct ice.
3. Plastic-edge crack and lift hardened pieces.
4. Hidden-seam Goo Gone test on dark / raw denim.
5. Goo Gone cloth tamp for residue, outside-in.
6. Dawn damp-cloth + cool machine wash + AIR-DRY.
products: Plastic card / plastic spoon / dull butter knife
Goo Gone / citrus solvent (hidden seam test on indigo)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold for freeze + cool throughout cleaning
warnings: Never pick at warm gum — spreads polymer into weave
Never direct ice on denim — water spotting
NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly
Hidden-seam test before Goo Gone on dark / raw denim
Always blot, never rub — denim halos under scrubbing

homeSolutions.instruction: The instinct is to scrape warm gum off with a fingernail or kitchen knife. Don't. Warm gum is soft and spreads — picking pushes the polymer deeper into the denim weave and makes the stain wider. Stop, freeze it brittle first, then it lifts off cleanly.

Put ice in a sealed plastic bag and place it on the gum for 15 to 20 minutes. NEVER direct ice on denim — direct water causes ring spotting that's its own stain. The gum is ready when it's hard and brittle.

Use a plastic card, plastic spoon, or dull butter knife. Crack the frozen gum with the edge — most of it lifts off in pieces. Don't gouge the denim — flat-edge only. Most surface gum is gone at this point.

Before any solvent on dark or indigo denim: dab a drop of Goo Gone on an inside seam, blot with a white cloth. If indigo transfers, skip the Goo Gone step — go straight to Dawn. If the cloth comes up clean, Goo Gone is safe on the visible spot.

Apply Goo Gone (citrus solvent, orange bottle) to a clean white cloth — never pour on denim. Tamp the residue from the top, flip the cloth as it picks up sticky transfer. Outside-in. 2 passes. Open windows — Goo Gone is flammable, no flame nearby.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn Damp Cloth and Cool Wash
homeSolutions.instruction: Dawn worked into a damp cloth, press the spot for 5 minutes to clear Goo Gone residue. Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY. Inspect dry — wet denim sometimes hides residual stickiness that reappears once dry.

steps: 1. Don't pick at warm gum. Stop, freeze first.
2. Ice-bag freeze 15–20 min — never direct ice on denim.
3. Plastic-edge crack and lift hardened pieces.
4. Hidden-seam Goo Gone test on dark / raw denim.
5. Goo Gone cloth tamp for residue, outside-in, 2 passes.
6. Dawn damp cloth + cool machine wash + AIR-DRY.
products: Goo Gone / citrus solvent (hidden test on indigo)
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop on raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim from minute one (pro). Stop if hidden-seam test shows indigo loss — Dawn-only finish. Stop if gum has been ironed or hot-dried (often permanent indent).
warnings: Never pick at warm gum — spreads polymer
Always blot, never rub — denim halos

fiberSensitivities: Indigo / dark denim: hidden-seam Goo Gone test mandatory
Raw / selvage denim: pro from minute one
Light-wash denim: most forgiving — full home protocol applicable
Stretch denim with elastane: cool only
Black overdyed denim: pro — color-loss risk on solvent

materialWarnings: Freeze before lift — never pick at warm gum
Always blot, never rub — denim halos
Raw / selvage denim = pro from minute one

escalation.when: Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim. Hidden-seam test shows indigo loss. Gum has been ironed or hot-dried (often permanent indent). Halo ring already forming around the spot. Polymer worked deep into the weave from prior picking attempts.
escalation.whatToTell: "Gum on [light-wash / dark / indigo / raw / selvage] denim, age [fresh / sat-on / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [ice freeze + plastic-edge lift + Goo Gone cloth tamp + Dawn + cool wash]. No hot, no chlorine. [Residual stickiness / indent / indigo loss / halo]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with denim experience

contextQuestions: Light-wash, dark, raw / selvage, or black-overdyed denim?
Fresh, sat-on, or already hot-dried / ironed?
Has anyone picked at it warm?
Visible polymer or just sticky residue?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: The freeze-and-lift method is high-success on washable denim. The recurring trap is picking at warm gum, which spreads the polymer into the weave and turns a small stain into a wider one. Indigo loss on dark denim is the second risk; hidden-seam test before any solvent. Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim goes to pro because the dye is more fragile and any solvent + scrubbing risks visible damage.
